The mission will study the impact of climate change and pollution on the Arctic Ocean. For more, we're joined by Marcel Babin, Scientific Director of the Tara Polaris I expedition.

items.title: Kuwait, Bahrain respond to Iranian attacks amid growing escalation

The escalation comes after the US Embassy in Jordan urged American citizens, in a social media post, to avoid Aqaba's airport and seaport because of a specific and credible threat. items.title: Cuban dissident artist arrives in US after 5-years in prison
items.description: A hero's welcome for Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara as he arrived in Miami following his release from prison in Cuba. The dissident artist accepted exile after the United States granted him humanitarian parole. Otero Alcántara co-founded the San Isidro Movement, a collective of Havana-based artists, writers, and musicians that protests against the Cuban government. In 2021, he was arrested in Havana during anti-government demonstrations and was later sentenced to five years in prison on charges of public disorder, contempt, and disrespect for national symbols. Carla Colomé Santiago, a Cuban journalist based in Miami, shares her insights.

items.title: Russia pounds Kyiv in major ballistic missile attack
items.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Russia battered Ukraine's capital Kyiv overnight into Sunday local time, with one of its biggest ballistic missile barrages of the war.

items.title: Watch: What to know as Genoa bridge disaster trial ends
items.description: More than 50 defendants have been on trial in Italy over the 2018 bridge collapse which killed 43 people.

items.title: England stun France in goal fest for third place
items.description: England finish third after dominating in the first half. France managed to claw its way back into the game, but did not manage to find the win for Didier Deschamps' last match as French manager. Mbappé becomes the highest scoring player in world cup history, and Michael Olise matches Lionel Messi's all-time assist record. Meanwhile, Spain are hoping to get another star on their jersey, whilst Argentina is looking to achieve back-to-back world cup victories.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Outback tour company sues vehicle manufacturer over fatal NT rollover
items.description: An Alice Springs tour company is suing a vehicle manufacturer for negligence and breach of contract following a bus crash that killed a 69-year-old man in 2022.

items.title: Tinubu’s $1 trillion GDP target and Rufai Oseni’s tears, By ‘Tope Fasua
items.description: We live in an impressionable world, even though literacy has continued to improve the world over – Nigeria inclusive. One of the downsides of social media is the velocity of information being pushed at everyone – information and data that no mind can fully process. items.title: Ukraine lawmakers approve Koretskyi as new PM amid protests
items.description: It is President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's second Cabinet shakeup in a year. He faces criticism for dismissing reformist Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

items.title: Could Russia be about to lose its grip on chess leadership?
items.description: Russians have long pulled the strings when it comes to the governing body of international chess. But now two Germans are vying for FIDE's top job.

items.title: Curious case of absent US senator reignites elderly politicians debate
items.description: Washington's rumour mill is hyperactive at the best of times. But an elderly senator's lengthy absence from Congress is cranking it into frenzied overdrive.

items.title: DRC: Uvira's displaced lose hope as US ultimatum on Rwanda troops expires
items.description: The US ultimatum for Rwandan troops to withdraw from eastern DRC expired on Wednesday but there’s been no let up in the fighting. Now thousands of displaced families in Uvira are seeing their hopes of returning home slip away.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Messi: 'Historical context' makes World Cup win over England 'special'
items.description: Messi says he's looking forward to facing Spain in the World Cup final on Sunday; Skipper will face the country where he spent most of his career, at Barcelona. Leer más

items.title: New Bundesliga TV deal in US prioritizes reach over money
items.description: The Bundesliga has a new home on American screens after Germany's top football league left ESPN. The new deal is a sign of the times.

items.title: How new PM Andy Burnham will change UK's international role
items.description: From northwest England to the global stage: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new prime minister on Monday. How will the former mayor of Manchester tackle international affairs?

items.title: SpaceX targets next week for another Starship launch attempt as shares slide on abort
Germany may make it illegal to deny Israel's right to exist
items.description: Germany's upper house of parliament, the Bundesrat, has introduced a bill seeking to ban the questioning of Israel's right to exist. It is now up to the lower house to decide whether to turn it into law.

items.title: Gaza cheers for Spain as World Cup final anticipation mounts
items.description: Football fans in Gaza express support for Spain in showdown with Argentina, citing Spanish solidarity with Palestinians.

items.title: East-West border death zone now Germany's biodiversity oasis
items.description: Many endangered species now live in the area that once formed the border between East and West Germany. Environment Minister Carsten Schneider recently visited the "Green Belt," and DW went along for the ride.

items.title: U.S. airstrikes on Iran appear to have damaged Chabahar port; India says no damage to its terminal
items.description: Chabahar port has been a repeated target of American airstrikes; India operates one of the two terminals at the port

items.title: Tensions as Guinea-Bissau freezes ties with Cape Verde over claims of interference
items.description: Praia earlier this week requested that Bissau swiftly release the country’s main opposition leader Domingos Simões Pereira.

items.title: Memorial unveiled for Romanian diplomat who saved Jews from Nazis
items.description: Florin Manoliu smuggled hundreds of protective passports past Nazi guards and helped expose Auschwitz's horrors to the Allied press; British Cemetery in Buenos Aires honours Romainian diplomat who found refuge in Bahía Blanca with new monument. Leer más

items.title: Landslide in China's Chongqing city kills 8, leaves 34 missing
items.description: Water, electricity and gas supplies were cut off within a 1-km (0.6-mile) radius of the landslide to prevent further disruptions. Over 800 rescuers were on the site

items.title: Spain or Argentina, who will win the FIFA World Cup? Ex-player predictions

items.title: Tunisia reels in extreme heat as temperatures near 50°C
items.description: An exceptionally intense heat wave swept across Tunisia, pushing temperatures to nearly 50 degrees Celsius and breaking historical records in multiple regions, local media reported on Saturday

items.title: Marathon runners compete on drifting sea ice at North Pole
items.description: Organisers say 23 runners from seven countries took part in races at the Arctic's geographic North Pole.

items.title: India launches first hydrogen-powered passenger train to cut emissions
items.description: India launches its first hydrogen-powered passenger train, NaMo Green Rail, boosting clean rail transport and cutting diesel emissions.

items.title: "Shangwe, le bal": a dance project full of joy
items.description: On a sweltering afternoon in the French city of Lyon, around 60 dancers fully ignored the heat to immerse themselves in a festive performance brought to them by Kenyan-American choreographer and dancer Wanjiru Kamuyu.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: German tenants need better heat protection
items.description: Heat waves are becoming longer, hotter and more frequent. Record temperatures have left many tenants struggling. Now Chancellor Friedrich Merz has said the building law needs to be amended.

items.title: Germany news: Minister warns of high terror threat
items.description: Germany's interior minister has warned of a heightened security threat, saying an attack could be imminent. Meanwhile, a train security guard was critically injured after falling from a moving train. DW has the latest.

items.title: Argentina stun England with dramatic comeback to reach World Cup final
items.description: Lautaro Martínez scores 92nd minute winner as World Cup holders Argentina perform a stunning comeback to beat England 2-1 and set up a final with European champions Spain. Leer más

items.title: Senegal: Faye, ex-president Macky Sall hold high-stakes meeting
items.description: Senegal's ex-president Macky Sall, who is campaigning to become the next UN secretary-general, held a meeting in Dakar Friday with his successor, a visit criticised by the former leader's detractors

items.description: Football fans are putting their faith in good luck rituals ahead of Sunday's World Cup final. But these wacky habits are no joke to those who practice them. Leer más

items.title: US bombs Iran for eighth-straight night
items.description: The US has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran after two American soldiers were killed by Iranian strikes in Jordan.

items.title: Expert says new cyber law shows Vic government is out of touch with reality
items.description: Jacinta Allan is pledging nation-leading laws forcing tech giants to reveal the identity of trolls, and make it easier for parents to sue tech giants that cause psychiatric harm to their children.

items.title: Why did Jens Spahn's surrogacy spark debate in Germany?
items.description: Jens Spahn, a key ally of Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is stepping down amid a controversy surrounding his decision to have a child via a surrogate mother. He had opposed legalizing surrogacy in Germany.

items.title: Ethiopians march against alleged forced mobilisation in Tigray
items.description: Thousands rally in Ethiopia's capital Addis Ababa to protest the alleged forced recruitment of civilians in the Tigray region by Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) forces, the breakaway region's main political party, amid months of escalating tensions with Ethiopia's federal government

items.title: Control, threats, disfiguring surgery: My life inside Jeffrey Epstein's 'cult'
items.description: "Anya" gives the BBC a rare account of how sex-criminal financier Epstein lured and abused his “assistants”.

items.title: Indian-origin woman sentenced to two years jail over Covid business loan fraud in U.K.
items.description: Rupali Wagh, a businesswoman, based in Wales, pleaded guilty to five counts of fraud when she appeared at Cardiff Crown Court in November

items.title: Algerian prime minister visits survivors after deadly fire at orphanage
items.description: Algerian Prime Minister Sifi Ghrieb speaks with survivors after a fire burning at an orphanage in the country's capital has killed at least 11 people, including children, according to authorities.

items.title: Monaco assassination plot takes a new turn
items.description: A woman suspected of plotting to assassinate a Ukrainian-Cypriot businessman in Monaco has been found dead in Ukraine. Two men with ties to Ukraine's intelligence services have now been brought into custody.

items.title: Brad Pitt's children are dropping their dad's surname
items.description: Hannah had used her mother's maiden name on her social media for years before she legally made the change.
